pi_items.source: docker-compose.yml line 10 mounts /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web:/config in the web container; setup_task.sh line 14 and export_result.sh line 7 both explicitly reference this path
task.json line 5 description: 'join a new meeting room named 'QuietMeeting' to verify the policy works'; this is a valid arbitrary room name for Jitsi Meet (any room name can be joined)

Jitsi Meet official documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) confirms 'startWithAudioMuted' is a real boolean configuration option: 'Start calls with audio muted. This option is only applied locally.' Also confirmed in Jitsi Meet GitHub issues and community forums.

Jitsi Meet official documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) confirms 'startWithVideoMuted' is a real boolean configuration option: 'Start calls with video muted. Only applied locally.' Also confirmed in Jitsi Meet GitHub issues and community forums.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures the Jitsi Meet server to enforce a 'quiet start' policy by modifying the web configuration file at /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js (verified by docker-compose.yml volume mount and task scripts). The configuration requires setting two real Jitsi Meet settings: startWithAudioMuted and startWithVideoMuted to true (both are verified official Jitsi Meet configuration options documented in the Jitsi handbook). The task uses a synthetic Jitsi Meet environment (Docker container running jitsi/web:stable-9753) and targets an arbitrary test meeting room named 'QuietMeeting'. When properly configured, these settings cause all users joining the meeting to have both microphone and camera disabled by default.
